description_wiki: Kintarō Hayakawa (June 10, 1886 – November 23, 1973), known professionally as Sessue Hayakawa, was a Japanese actor.

description: city in San Bernardino County, California, United States
description_wiki: Big Bear Lake is a city in San Bernardino County, California, United States, located in the San Bernardino Mountains along the south shore of Big Bear Lake, and surrounded by the San Bernardino National Forest.

description: president of the United States from 1961 to 1963 (1917–1963)
description_wiki: John Fitzgerald Kennedy (May 29, 1917 – November 22, 1963), also known as JFK, was the 35th president of the United States, serving from 1961 until his assassination in 1963.

description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.
The Plymouth Barracuda is a two-door pony car that was manufactured by Chrysler Corporation from 1964 through 1974 model years.
Datsun (UK: , US: ) was a Japanese automobile manufacturer brand owned by Nissan.

description: city in Oklahoma, United States and county seat of Tulsa County
description_wiki: Tulsa (  TUL-sə) is the second-most-populous city in the U.S. state of Oklahoma and the 48th-most populous city in the United States.

description: city in Osage and Tulsa counties, Oklahoma, United States
description_wiki: Sand Springs is a city in Osage, Creek and Tulsa counties in the U.S. state of Oklahoma.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description: art museum and school in Chicago, United States
description_wiki: The Art Institute of Chicago is a private, nonprofit art museum in Grant Park, Chicago, Illinois, United States.

description: public park at the center of Manhattan in New York City, New York, United States
description_wiki: Central Park is an urban park between the Upper West Side and Upper East Side neighborhoods of Manhattan in New York City, and the first landscaped park in the United States.
